name: Deep Sleep Component
description: The `deep_sleep` component can be used to automatically enter a deep sleep mode on the ESP8266/ESP32/BK72xx/nRF52 after a certain amount of time. This is especially useful with nodes that operate on batteries and therefore need to conserve as much energy as possible.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Esp32 Ext1 Wakeup
config_entries.description: Use the EXT1 wakeup source of the ESP32 to wake from deep sleep to wake up on multiple pins. This cannot be used together with wakeup pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: The mode to use for the wakeup source. Must be one of:

options.label: wake up when any selected pin is LOW (ESP32‑C5/C6/C61/H2/P4/S2/S3 only)
wake up when all selected pins are LOW (ESP32 only)
wake up when any selected pin is HIGH

config_entries.label: Pins
config_entries.description: The pins to wake up on.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Run Duration
config_entries.description: The time duration the node should be active, i.e. run code. Only on ESP32, instead of time, it is possible to specify run duration according to the wakeup reason from deep-sleep:

config_entries.label: Default
config_entries.description: default run duration for timer wakeup and any unspecified wakeup reason.

config_entries.label: GPIO Wakeup Reason
config_entries.description: run duration if woken up by GPIO.

config_entries.label: Touch Wakeup Reason
config_entries.description: run duration if woken up by touch.

config_entries.label: Sleep Duration
config_entries.description: The time duration to stay in deep sleep mode. On BK72xx, the maximum is 36 hours. On Zephyr, the maximum is 49 days.

config_entries.label: Touch Wakeup
config_entries.description: Only on ESP32. Use a touch event to wakeup from deep sleep. To be able to wakeup from a touch event, [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/esp32_touch#binary-sensor) must be configured properly.

config_entries.label: Wakeup Pin
config_entries.description: Only on ESP32/BK72xx. A single pin to wake up to once in deep sleep mode. Use the inverted property to wake up to LOW signals. If you want to specify multiple wake-up pins (BK72xx only) specify them under `wakeup_pin` using a list of entries consisting of:

config_entries.label: Wakeup Pin Mode
config_entries.description: Only on ESP32/BK72xx. Specify how to handle waking up from a `wakeup_pin` if the wakeup pin is already in the state with which it would wake up when attempting to enter deep sleep. See [Wakeup Pin Mode](https://esphome.io/components/deep_sleep#deep_sleep-esp32_wakeup_pin_mode). Defaults to `IGNORE`
